title: Require Service oRPC Relationships
forbids: missing or noncanonical ordinary TypeScript anchor exports; namespace/default/noncanonical root composition imports; an imported module omitted from its canonical contract or router composition; a missing or second root implementer; a wrong implement argument or unused implementation result; non-native middleware definitions or use sites; a module branch inconsistent with its canonical directory key; module imports of root context; a router interior that exports an implemented router instead of a plain map; a noncanonical or unused authored interior-router import; non-destructured native handlers; and leaf access, destructuring, assignment, or aliasing of initial dependencies, the root request, or root authentication projections
why: One contract-first oRPC lineage carries initial context through native middleware into narrow execution context. Modules inherit the root implementer, project only their own dependencies, and leave leaf procedures unable to recover root authority. A split router interior composes a plain procedure map imported as procedures while router.ts remains the module boundary. Contract schema and public-error ownership are enforced by their dedicated packets; ordinary anchor export syntax stays in this Grit-owned source relation.
remediate: Import module contracts and routers through the exact named relative alias form, compose every imported alias in its owning router, call the native implementer once with contract in impl.ts, derive service from that result, attach only native oRPC middleware, derive modules from their canonical root branch, export only a plain map from an optional router/index.ts, import that map as procedures in router.ts and pass it to module.router, and project initial dependencies before leaf execution.
message: Service source must preserve one native oRPC contract, context, middleware, module, and router lineage.
